paper.title: A Game of Thrones — Chapter 5 (JON)
paper.author: George R. R. Martin
paper.reader_mode: spoiler-free close reading; English personal names

blocks.original_text: There were times—not many, but a few—when Jon Snow was glad he was a bastard. As he filled his wine cup once more from a passing flagon, it struck him that this might be one of them.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon少数几次庆幸自己是bastard：被安排在低处反而让他能喝酒并从容观察王室宴会。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 开篇把污名写成偶尔有利的观察位置；Jon的自由来自被排除，而非真正被接纳。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Jon是Ned的私生子，宴会礼法没有让他与Stark婚生子女及王室同坐高台。

blocks.original_text: He settled back in his place on the bench among the younger squires and drank. The sweet, fruity taste of summerwine filled his mouth and brought a smile to his lips.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 他坐在年轻squires中不断饮酒，甜酒比平时更浓，使感官和情绪逐渐失去约束。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段借Jon在低处观看高台，展示宴会礼仪如何把合法子女、bastard、王室和家臣排列成可见等级。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: The Great Hall of Winterfell was hazy with smoke and heavy with the smell of roasted meat and fresh-baked bread. Its grey stone walls were draped with banners. White, gold, crimson: the direwolf of Stark, Baratheon’s crowned stag, the lion of Lannister. A singer was playing the high harp and reciting a ballad, but down at this end of the hall his voice could scarcely be heard above the roar of the fire, the clangor of pewter plates and cups, and the low mutter of a hundred drunken conversations.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Great Hall充满烟、烤肉、面包、啤酒和人声，宴会的丰盛与混乱由多种感官共同呈现。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段借Jon在低处观看高台，展示宴会礼仪如何把合法子女、bastard、王室和家臣排列成可见等级。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: It was the fourth hour of the welcoming feast laid for the king. Jon’s brothers and sisters had been seated with the royal children, beneath the raised platform where Lord and Lady Stark hosted the king and queen. In honor of the occasion, his lord father would doubtless permit each child a glass of wine, but no more than that. Down here on the benches, there was no one to stop Jon drinking as much as he had a thirst for.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 欢迎宴已进行四小时；Jon的兄弟姐妹坐在高台，而他因身份不能与家人同席。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 高台座位把家庭关系空间化：兄弟姐妹在上方，Jon与squires在下方，身份差异无需解释便可看见。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: And he was finding that he had a man’s thirst, to the raucous delight of the youths around him, who urged him on every time he drained a glass. They were fine company, and Jon relished the stories they were telling, tales of battle and bedding and the hunt. He was certain that his companions were more entertaining than the king’s offspring. He had sated his curiosity about the visitors when they made their entrance. The procession had passed not a foot from the place he had been given on the bench, and Jon had gotten a good long look at them all.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 周围青年因他酒量而起哄，Jon把继续饮酒当成接近“成年男人”的证明。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段借Jon在低处观看高台，展示宴会礼仪如何把合法子女、bastard、王室和家臣排列成可见等级。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: His lord father had come first, escorting the queen. She was as beautiful as men said. A jeweled tiara gleamed amidst her long golden hair, its emeralds a perfect match for the green of her eyes. His father helped her up the steps to the dais and led her to her seat, but the queen never so much as looked at him. Even at fourteen, Jon could see through her smile.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Ned陪Cersei入席；Jon承认王后美丽，也敏锐察觉她礼貌笑容中的冷淡。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Jon能从礼貌笑容中读出冷淡，延续他作为边缘者必须发展出的观察能力。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Next had come King Robert himself, with Lady Stark on his arm. The king was a great disappointment to Jon. His father had talked of him often: the peerless Robert Baratheon, demon of the Trident, the fiercest warrior of the realm, a giant among princes. Jon saw only a fat man, red-faced under his beard, sweating through his silks. He walked like a man half in his cups.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Robert与Catelyn随后出现；传说中的英雄如今肥胖醉态，让Jon深感失望。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Robert的外表让Jon的英雄想象破裂，与Chapter 4中Ned的震惊形成不同年龄视角的呼应。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Robert年轻时以强大战士闻名；Jon此前只从Ned的讲述建立英雄想象。

blocks.original_text: After them came the children. Little Rickon first, managing the long walk with all the dignity a three-year-old could muster. Jon had to urge him on when he stopped to visit. Close behind came Robb, in grey wool trimmed with white, the Stark colors. He had the Princess Myrcella on his arm. She was a wisp of a girl, not quite eight, her hair a cascade of golden curls under a jeweled net. Jon noticed the shy looks she gave Robb as they passed between the tables and the timid way she smiled at him. He decided she was insipid. Robb didn’t even have the sense to realize how stupid she was; he was grinning like a fool.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 王室与Stark孩子依次入席；三岁的Rickon努力保持尊严，使正式礼仪带一点儿童喜剧。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段借Jon在低处观看高台，展示宴会礼仪如何把合法子女、bastard、王室和家臣排列成可见等级。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: His half sisters escorted the royal princes. Arya was paired with plump young Tommen, whose white-blond hair was longer than hers. Sansa, two years older, drew the crown prince, Joffrey Baratheon. He was twelve, younger than Jon or Robb, but taller than either, to Jon’s vast dismay. Prince Joffrey had his sister’s hair and his mother’s deep green eyes. A thick tangle of blond curls dripped down past his golden choker and high velvet collar. Sansa looked radiant as she walked beside him, but Jon did not like Joffrey’s pouty lips or the bored, disdainful way he looked at Winterfell’s Great Hall.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Arya陪Tommen，Sansa陪Joffrey；Jon注意到Joffrey的外貌与傲慢，也知道Sansa被他吸引。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段借Jon在低处观看高台，展示宴会礼仪如何把合法子女、bastard、王室和家臣排列成可见等级。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: He was more interested in the pair that came behind him: the queen’s brothers, the Lannisters of Casterly Rock. The Lion and the Imp; there was no mistaking which was which. Ser Jaime Lannister was twin to Queen Cersei; tall and golden, with flashing green eyes and a smile that cut like a knife. He wore crimson silk, high black boots, a black satin cloak. On the breast of his tunic, the lion of his House was embroidered in gold thread, roaring its defiance. They called him the Lion of Lannister to his face and whispered “Kingslayer” behind his back.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon更关注王后兄弟：Jaime步伐与外貌都像故事中的英雄骑士。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Jaime先以视觉完成骑士理想，读者此刻只知道Jon看到的外表，不应提前代入后文评价。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Jaime Lannister是Cersei的双胞胎弟弟和Kingsguard成员。

blocks.original_text: Jon found it hard to look away from him. This is what a king should look like, he thought to himself as the man passed.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon难以移开目光，心中认为Jaime才符合国王应有的外表。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段借Jon在低处观看高台，展示宴会礼仪如何把合法子女、bastard、王室和家臣排列成可见等级。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Then he saw the other one, waddling along half-hidden by his brother’s side. Tyrion Lannister, the youngest of Lord Tywin’s brood and by far the ugliest. All that the gods had given to Cersei and Jaime, they had denied Tyrion. He was a dwarf, half his brother’s height, struggling to keep pace on stunted legs. His head was too large for his body, with a brute’s squashed-in face beneath a swollen shelf of brow. One green eye and one black one peered out from under a lank fall of hair so blond it seemed white. Jon watched him with fascination.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 随后出现的Tyrion身材矮小、比例异常，与Jaime并肩时形成极强反差。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 兄弟并行使身体差异无法被忽略；文本先展示Jon的观看，而不是要求读者立即下道德判断。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Tyrion Lannister是Cersei与Jaime的弟弟，因dwarf身体特征被称作Imp。

blocks.original_text: The last of the high lords to enter were his uncle, Benjen Stark of the Night’s Watch, and his father’s ward, young Theon Greyjoy. Benjen gave Jon a warm smile as he went by. Theon ignored him utterly, but there was nothing new in that. After all had been seated, toasts were made, thanks were given and returned, and then the feasting began.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 最后入席的是Benjen Stark和Theon Greyjoy；Benjen向Jon微笑，Theon照例完全忽略他。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段借Jon在低处观看高台，展示宴会礼仪如何把合法子女、bastard、王室和家臣排列成可见等级。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Jon had started drinking then, and he had not stopped.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon从那时开始饮酒，一直没有停下。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段借Jon在低处观看高台，展示宴会礼仪如何把合法子女、bastard、王室和家臣排列成可见等级。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Something rubbed against his leg beneath the table. Jon saw red eyes staring up at him. “Hungry again?” he asked. There was still half a honeyed chicken in the center of the table. Jon reached out to tear off a leg, then had a better idea. He knifed the bird whole and let the carcass slide to the floor between his legs. Ghost ripped into it in savage silence. His brothers and sisters had not been permitted to bring their wolves to the banquet, but there were more curs than Jon could count at this end of the hall, and no one had said a word about his pup. He told himself he was fortunate in that too.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Ghost在桌下蹭Jon的腿索食；Jon把鸡肉悄悄递给他。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: His eyes stung. Jon rubbed at them savagely, cursing the smoke. He swallowed another gulp of wine and watched his direwolf devour the chicken.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon眼睛刺痛，声称是烟造成并猛擦眼睛，实际也在压制被排除在家庭高台外的情绪。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `cursing the smoke`是自我掩饰；Jon宁愿把眼泪归因于烟，也不愿承认被家庭排除造成的痛。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Dogs moved between the tables, trailing after the serving girls. One of them, a black mongrel bitch with long yellow eyes, caught a scent of the chicken. She stopped and edged under the bench to get a share. Jon watched the confrontation. The bitch growled low in her throat and moved closer. Ghost looked up, silent, and fixed the dog with those hot red eyes. The bitch snapped an angry challenge. She was three times the size of the direwolf pup. Ghost did not move. He stood over his prize and opened his mouth, baring his fangs. The bitch tensed, barked again, then thought better of this fight. She turned and slunk away, with one last defiant snap to save her pride. Ghost went back to his meal.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 一只黑色杂种狗试图抢食，Ghost无声露齿便让更大的狗退走。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Ghost不吠不扑，只露齿便让大狗退开；无声控制与Jon在人群中的压抑形成对应。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Jon grinned and reached under the table to ruffle the shaggy white fur. The direwolf looked up at him, nipped gently at his hand, then went back to eating.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon为Ghost的镇定与威慑力高兴，抚摸白毛，Ghost以红眼回应。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Is this one of the direwolves I’ve heard so much of?” a familiar voice asked close at hand.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 熟悉声音询问这是否就是传闻中的direwolf。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Jon looked up happily as his uncle Ben put a hand on his head and ruffled his hair much as Jon had ruffled the wolf’s. “Yes,” he said. “His name is Ghost.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon高兴抬头，Benjen像Jon摸Ghost那样揉乱他的头发，亲近动作形成呼应。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: One of the squires interrupted the bawdy story he’d been telling to make room at the table for their lord’s brother. Benjen Stark straddled the bench with long legs and took the wine cup out of Jon’s hand. “Summerwine,” he said after a taste. “Nothing so sweet. How many cups have you had, Jon?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 年轻squire让座给Benjen，他先称赞Ghost，又带笑指出Jon喝醉了。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Jon smiled.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon只是微笑，没有否认。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Ben Stark laughed. “As I feared. Ah, well. I believe I was younger than you the first time I got truly and sincerely drunk.” He snagged a roasted onion, dripping brown with gravy, from a nearby trencher and bit into it. It crunched.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen笑说自己第一次真正喝醉时可能比Jon更小，并随手拿起一只浸满肉汁的烤洋葱。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: His uncle was sharp-featured and gaunt as a mountain crag, but there was always a hint of laughter in his blue-grey eyes. He dressed in black, as befitted a man of the Night’s Watch. Tonight it was rich black velvet, with high leather boots and a wide belt with a silver buckle. A heavy silver chain was looped round his neck. Benjen watched Ghost with amusement as he ate his onion. “A very quiet wolf,” he observed.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen外貌瘦削如山岩，黑衣朴素，却始终带着让Jon亲近的笑意。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “He’s not like the others,” Jon said. “He never makes a sound. That’s why I named him Ghost. That, and because he’s white. The others are all dark, grey or black.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon解释Ghost不同于其他幼崽：他从不出声，因此得名；白色毛皮也与灰黑色同窝幼崽不同。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Ghost的名字来自沉默，而非颜色；`born with his eyes open`又强化他异常敏锐的形象。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Jon的白色direwolf名为Ghost；他无声、红眼，并比其他幼崽更早睁眼。

blocks.original_text: “There are still direwolves beyond the Wall. We hear them on our rangings.” Benjen Stark gave Jon a long look. “Don’t you usually eat at table with your brothers?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen说长城外仍有direwolves，巡逻时能听见；他随后询问Jon为何不坐在家人旁边。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Benjen是Ned的弟弟和守夜人游骑兵，会越过长城执行rangings。

blocks.original_text: “Most times,” Jon answered in a flat voice. “But tonight Lady Stark thought it might give insult to the royal family to seat a bastard among them.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon平静回答Catelyn担心bastard与王室孩子同席会冒犯王后。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Catelyn没有安排Jon与王室同席；本段呈现Jon对决定的理解。

blocks.original_text: “I see.” His uncle glanced over his shoulder at the raised table at the far end of the hall. “My brother does not seem very festive tonight.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen看向远处高台，转而评论自己的哥哥Ned今晚似乎并不快乐。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Jon had noticed that too. A bastard had to learn to notice things, to read the truth that people hid behind their eyes. His father was observing all the courtesies, but there was tightness in him that Jon had seldom seen before. He said little, looking out over the hall with hooded eyes, seeing nothing. Two seats away, the king had been drinking heavily all night. His broad face was flushed behind his great black beard. He made many a toast, laughed loudly at every jest, and attacked each dish like a starving man, but beside him the queen seemed as cold as an ice sculpture. “The queen is angry too,” Jon told his uncle in a low, quiet voice. “Father took the king down to the crypts this afternoon. The queen didn’t want him to go.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon早已注意到；作为bastard，他学会观察人们隐藏在言语后的真实态度。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `read the truth that people hid behind their eyes`定义Jon的生存技能：没有明确地位的人必须比别人更会观察。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Benjen gave Jon a careful, measuring look. “You don’t miss much, do you, Jon? We could use a man like you on the Wall.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen认真打量Jon，称赞他的观察力，并说守夜人需要他这样的人。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Benjen一句称赞被Jon听成加入守夜人的许可，显示他早已等待一个归属出口。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Jon swelled with pride. “Robb is a stronger lance than I am, but I’m the better sword, and Hullen says I sit a horse as well as anyone in the castle.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon因称赞而自豪，列举自己在长剑、骑术等训练中胜过Robb的地方。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Notable achievements.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen用简短的“显著成就”回应，语气带着成人对少年自夸的保留。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段通过Ghost与Benjen暂时缓解孤独，同时让Jon的观察力、骄傲和饮酒后的冲动逐步显现。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Take me with you when you go back to the Wall,” Jon said in a sudden rush. “Father will give me leave to go if you ask him, I know he will.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon突然请求随Benjen返回长城，认为父亲会同意他加入守夜人。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 请求看似突然，实际是长期思考借酒说出口；真正突然的是表达，不是愿望。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Uncle Benjen studied his face carefully. “The Wall is a hard place for a boy, Jon.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen仔细审视他，警告长城对一个男孩而言非常艰苦。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “I am almost a man grown,” Jon protested. “I will turn fifteen on my next name day, and Maester Luwin says bastards grow up faster than other children.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon抗议自己即将十五岁，已接近成年，并以学识为证。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “That’s true enough,” Benjen said with a downward twist of his mouth. He took Jon’s cup from the table, filled it fresh from a nearby pitcher, and drank down a long swallow.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen承认他懂得不少，却拿走酒杯，暗示酒精正在夸大他的自信。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Daeren Targaryen was only fourteen when he conquered Dorne,” Jon said. The Young Dragon was one of his heroes.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon举Daeron Targaryen十四岁征服Dorne为例，证明少年也能建立功业。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Daeron I被称为Young Dragon，十四岁征服Dorne，但统治短暂且伤亡巨大。

blocks.original_text: “A conquest that lasted a summer,” his uncle pointed out. “Your Boy King lost ten thousand men taking the place, and another fifty trying to hold it. Someone should have told him that war isn’t a game.” He took another sip of wine. “Also,” he said, wiping his mouth, “Daeren Targaryen was only eighteen when he died. Or have you forgotten that part?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen指出那场征服只维持一个夏天，付出大量生命，Daeron本人也早逝。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Benjen用伤亡与失败拆解少年英雄故事，区分被传颂的征服与真实代价。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “I forget nothing,” Jon boasted. The wine was making him bold. He tried to sit very straight, to make himself seem taller. “I want to serve in the Night’s Watch, Uncle.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon夸口自己记得一切；酒让他坐得更直、说得更大胆。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: He had thought on it long and hard, lying abed at night while his brothers slept around him. Robb would someday inherit Winterfell, would command great armies as the Warden of the North. Bran and Rickon would be Robb’s bannermen and rule holdfasts in his name. His sisters Arya and Sansa would marry the heirs of other great houses and go south as mistress of castles of their own. But what place could a bastard hope to earn?
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon长期思考加入守夜人：Robb将继承Winterfell，弟弟妹妹各有位置，只有自己没有明确归属。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Jon把兄弟姐妹的未来逐一列出，说明加入守夜人首先源于家中没有位置，而不只是浪漫荣誉。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “You don’t know what you’re asking, Jon. The Night’s Watch is a sworn brotherhood. We have no families. None of us will ever father sons. Our wife is duty. Our mistress is honor.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen说明守夜人是终身誓言兄弟会，成员不能有家庭，也不能生养子女。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 守夜人誓言的关键不是冒险，而是永久放弃配偶、土地和子女；Benjen把被Jon忽略的成本说清。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** 守夜人誓言要求终身服务，放弃婚姻、土地和子女。

blocks.original_text: “A bastard can have honor too,” Jon said. “I am ready to swear your oath.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon坚持bastard同样可以拥有荣誉，并说自己已准备发誓。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “You are a boy of fourteen,” Benjen said. “Not a man, not yet. Until you have known a woman, you cannot understand what you would be giving up.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen指出他只有十四岁，尚未真正了解女人与家庭，因此无法理解放弃它们的代价。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Benjen并非否定Jon能力，而是否定他在不了解人生选项时作出终身放弃的成熟度。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “I don’t care about that!” Jon said hotly.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon激烈声称自己不在乎那些生活。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “You might, if you knew what it meant,” Benjen said. “If you knew what the oath would cost you, you might be less eager to pay the price, son.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen回答，只有知道誓言会夺走什么，Jon才可能真正作出选择。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Jon felt anger rise inside him. “I’m not your son!”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon的愤怒爆发，喊出自己并不是Benjen的儿子。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Benjen Stark stood up. “More’s the pity.” He put a hand on Jon’s shoulder. “Come back to me after you’ve fathered a few bastards of your own, and we’ll see how you feel.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Benjen起身，以`More’s the pity`回应Jon不是自己儿子，并让Jon等亲自生过几个bastards后再谈。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `More’s the pity`同时表达亲情和界限：Benjen关心Jon，却不能以父亲身份替他决定。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Benjen是Jon的叔叔而非监护父亲，因此拒绝替他作出终身决定。

blocks.original_text: Jon trembled. “I will never father a bastard,” he said carefully. “Never!” He spat it out like venom.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon颤抖着发誓自己永远不会让任何孩子成为bastard，把这个身份的痛苦转成绝对誓言。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Jon厌恶的不是抽象私生制度，而是把自身痛苦复制给另一个孩子的可能。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Suddenly he realized that the table had fallen silent, and they were all looking at him. He felt the tears begin to well behind his eyes. He pushed himself to his feet.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 他突然发现整桌人都安静注视自己，羞耻感取代愤怒。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段围绕加入守夜人争论：Jon把它视为荣誉和归属，Benjen强调终身誓言中尚未被少年理解的损失。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “I must be excused,” he said with the last of his dignity. He whirled and bolted before they could see him cry. He must have drunk more wine than he had realized. His feet got tangled under him as he tried to leave, and he lurched sideways into a serving girl and sent a flagon of spiced wine crashing to the floor. Laughter boomed all around him, and Jon felt hot tears on his cheeks. Someone tried to steady him. He wrenched free of their grip and ran, half-blind, for the door. Ghost followed close at his heels, out into the night.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon维持最后一点尊严请求离席，随后转身逃离宴会。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 礼貌的`I must be excused`与随后的`bolted`形成反差：语言想保住尊严，身体只想逃跑。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: The yard was quiet and empty. A lone sentry stood high on the battlements of the inner wall, his cloak pulled tight around him against the cold. He looked bored and miserable as he huddled there alone, but Jon would have traded places with him in an instant. Otherwise the castle was dark and deserted. Jon had seen an abandoned holdfast once, a drear place where nothing moved but the wind and the stones kept silent about whatever people had lived there. Winterfell reminded him of that tonight.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 城堡院落安静空旷，只有守卫；冷夜与喧闹大厅形成强烈反差。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: The sounds of music and song spilled through the open windows behind him. They were the last things Jon wanted to hear. He wiped away his tears on the sleeve of his shirt, furious that he had let them fall, and turned to go.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 音乐从窗内传来，却使Jon更难受；他强忍眼泪，Ghost无声跟随。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 大厅的快乐声音在外面变成伤害，说明同一场宴会因归属位置不同而意义相反。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Boy,” a voice called out to him. Jon turned.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 一个声音叫住Jon，他转身寻找。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion Lannister was sitting on the ledge above the door to the Great Hall, looking for all the world like a gargoyle. The dwarf grinned down at him. “Is that animal a wolf?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion坐在大厅门上方的石檐，像怪兽雕像一样俯视Jon和Ghost。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Tyrion被比作gargoyle，看似怪物化，却是他主动选择的高处观察位置。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “A direwolf,” Jon said. “His name is Ghost.” He stared up at the little man, his disappointment suddenly forgotten. “What are you doing up there? Why aren’t you at the feast?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon介绍Ghost，并因Tyrion没有留在宴会而感到意外。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Too hot, too noisy, and I’d drunk too much wine,” the dwarf told him. “I learned long ago that it is considered rude to vomit on your brother. Might I have a closer look at your wolf?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion说宴会太热太吵、自己酒也喝多了，并用不该吐在哥哥身上的笑话解释离席，随后请求近看Ghost。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Jon hesitated, then nodded slowly. “Can you climb down, or shall I bring a ladder?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon迟疑后点头，并半认真地问Tyrion是否需要梯子下来。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, bleed that,” the little man said. He pushed himself off the ledge into empty air. Jon gasped, then watched with awe as Tyrion Lannister spun around in a tight ball, landed lightly on his hands, then vaulted backward onto his legs.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion直接从高处跃下，以空翻稳稳落地，打破Jon对其身体能力的预期。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 空翻立即修正Jon和读者对Tyrion身体的单一想象；受限不等于没有技巧。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Ghost backed away from him uncertainly.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Ghost不确定地后退。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: The dwarf dusted himself off and laughed. “I believe I’ve frightened your wolf. My apologies.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion拍去灰尘并道歉，笑说自己吓到了direwolf。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “He’s not scared,” Jon said. He knelt and called out. “Ghost, come here. Come on. That’s it.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon否认Ghost害怕，跪下呼唤并安抚他靠近。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: The wolf pup padded closer and nuzzled at Jon’s face, but he kept a wary eye on Tyrion Lannister, and when the dwarf reached out to pet him, he drew back and bared his fangs in a silent snarl. “Shy, isn’t he?” Lannister observed.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Ghost贴近Jon，却始终警惕盯着Tyrion，表现服从与自主戒备并存。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Ghost接受Jon命令但持续盯住Tyrion，动物反应保持独立，不只是主人的道具。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Sit, Ghost,” Jon commanded. “That’s it. Keep still.” He looked up at the dwarf. “You can touch him now. He won’t move until I tell him to. I’ve been training him.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon命Ghost坐下不动，并允许Tyrion触摸。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “I see,” Lannister said. He ruffled the snow-white fur between Ghost’s ears and said, “Nice wolf.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion抚摸Ghost白毛，称赞他是好狼。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “If I wasn’t here, he’d tear out your throat,” Jon said. It wasn’t actually true yet, but it would be.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon夸张声称自己若不在，Ghost会咬断Tyrion喉咙；他知道现在还做不到，却相信未来会。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “In that case, you had best stay close,” the dwarf said. He cocked his oversized head to one side and looked Jon over with his mismatched eyes. “I am Tyrion Lannister.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion顺着玩笑说Jon最好一直留在身边，介绍自己姓名，并用一双颜色不同的眼睛打量Jon。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “I know,” Jon said. He rose. Standing, he was taller than the dwarf. It made him feel strange.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon承认自己知道Tyrion身份；站起来后发现自己比对方高，产生复杂不适。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段从喧闹大厅转入冷清院落，并通过Tyrion的空翻与Ghost反应打破彼此的第一印象。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “You’re Ned Stark’s bastard, aren’t you?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion直接问Jon是否是Ned Stark的bastard。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Tyrion直接说出Jon最敏感的身份，开启两名家族边缘者之间不舒服却诚实的对话。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** `Snow`标示Jon在北方的私生身份；社会会公开以bastard称呼他。

blocks.original_text: Jon felt a coldness pass right through him. He pressed his lips together and said nothing.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: 这个词让Jon感到寒意，他抿紧嘴唇不回答。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段让Jon与Tyrion直接谈论bastard、dwarf和父亲的排斥，形成两种边缘身份的对照。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Did I offend you?” Lannister said. “Sorry. Dwarfs don’t have to be tactful. Generations of capering fools in motley have won me the right to dress badly and say any damn thing that comes into my head.” He grinned. “You are the bastard, though.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion问是否冒犯了他，并以自嘲说dwarfs无需委婉，因为多代小丑让他们获得这种特权。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Tyrion把社会对dwarf的嘲笑转化成不必遵守礼貌的自由，是与Jon“盔甲”策略相同的逻辑。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Lord Eddard Stark is my father,” Jon admitted stiffly.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon僵硬承认Lord Eddard Stark是自己的父亲。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段让Jon与Tyrion直接谈论bastard、dwarf和父亲的排斥，形成两种边缘身份的对照。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Lannister studied his face. “Yes,” he said. “I can see it. You have more of the north in you than your brothers.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion观察Jon面孔，认为他比其他兄弟更具北方特征。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段让Jon与Tyrion直接谈论bastard、dwarf和父亲的排斥，形成两种边缘身份的对照。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Half brothers,” Jon corrected. He was pleased by the dwarf’s comment, but he tried not to let it show.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon纠正他们是half brothers；他因这句评价高兴，却努力不表现。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段让Jon与Tyrion直接谈论bastard、dwarf和父亲的排斥，形成两种边缘身份的对照。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Let me give you some counsel, bastard,” Lannister said. “Never forget what you are, for surely the world will not. Make it your strength. Then it can never be your weakness. Armor yourself in it, and it will never be used to hurt you.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion劝Jon永远不要忘记bastard身份，应把它当作盔甲，使别人无法再用它伤害他。
blocks.reading_note_zh: `wear it like armor`不是让Jon喜欢污名，而是让他主动控制叙述，使羞辱失去突然袭击的力量。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: Jon was in no mood for anyone’s counsel. “What do you know about being a bastard?”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon正在气头上，反问Tyrion懂什么bastard处境。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段让Jon与Tyrion直接谈论bastard、dwarf和父亲的排斥，形成两种边缘身份的对照。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “All dwarfs are bastards in their father’s eyes.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion说在父亲眼中，所有dwarfs都是bastards。
blocks.reading_note_zh: Tyrion把dwarf与bastard类比的是父亲眼中的排斥，不是法律上的婚生身份。
blocks.background_note_zh: **文本事实：** Tyrion是婚生Lannister；他的类比针对父亲态度和社会排斥，并非法律身份。

blocks.original_text: “You are your mother’s trueborn son of Lannister.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon反驳Tyrion明明是Lannister合法婚生子。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段让Jon与Tyrion直接谈论bastard、dwarf和父亲的排斥，形成两种边缘身份的对照。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Am I?” the dwarf replied, sardonic. “Do tell my lord father. My mother died birthing me, and he’s never been sure.”
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion讽刺地让Jon去告诉自己的父亲；母亲因生他而死，而父亲似乎从未完全把他当作真正儿子。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 他用讽刺讲述母亲之死与父亲怨恨，笑话成为处理家庭创伤的防御机制。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “I don’t even know who my mother was,” Jon said.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Jon坦白自己甚至不知道母亲是谁。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 本段让Jon与Tyrion直接谈论bastard、dwarf和父亲的排斥，形成两种边缘身份的对照。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

blocks.original_text: “Some woman, no doubt. Most of them are.” He favored Jon with a rueful grin. “Remember this, boy. All dwarfs may be bastards, yet not all bastards need be dwarfs.” And with that he turned and sauntered back into the feast, whistling a tune. When he opened the door, the light from within threw his shadow clear across the yard, and for just a moment Tyrion Lannister stood tall as a king.
blocks.paragraph_explanation_zh: Tyrion用一句悖论式玩笑结束：dwarfs在父亲眼中或许都是bastards，但bastards不必把自己活得渺小；随后返回宴会。
blocks.reading_note_zh: 结尾重复核心建议，把一次偶遇变成Jon今后理解身份的可用原则。
blocks.background_note_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Jon的被排除感、守夜人选择或与Tyrion的交流。

glossary.note: 本书中守夜人的任务用语
`too many heirs` 暗示幼子继承机会有限
比冷淡更明确地表现优越感
常带不健康、骷髅般的感觉

glossary.translation: 受监护者；寄养的贵族子弟

glossary.note: 此处为Warden of the North头衔
历史语境中的法律身份，常带侮辱性

glossary.translation: 宣誓承担的；以誓言约束的

glossary.note: 此处指听见幼崽将死后的痛苦

glossary.translation: 婚生的；合法婚姻所生的

glossary.note: 此处可能带保留或讽刺
